title: An Act to Create Culturally Appropriate and Trauma-informed Housing and Recovery Services
summary: An Act to Create Culturally Appropriate and Trauma-informed Housing and Recovery Services

actions.action: Committee on Housing and Economic Development suggested and ordered printed.
The Bill was REFERRED to the Committee on HOUSING AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT.
Sent for concurrence. ORDERED SENT FORTHWITH.
The Bill was REFERRED to the Committee on HOUSING AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT  in concurrence
Work Session Held: TABLED
Work Session Held
Voted: Divided Report
Reported Out: OTP-AM/ONTP
Reports READ.
On motion of Representative GERE of Kennebunkport, the Majority Ought to Pass as Amended Report was ACCEPTED.
ROLL CALL NO. 424 (Yeas 75 - Nays 65 - Absent 9 - Excused 2)
The Bill was READ ONCE.
Committee Amendment "A" (H-626) was READ and ADOPTED.
Under suspension of the rules, the Bill was given its SECOND READING without REFERENCE to the Committee on Bills in the Second Reading.
The Bill was PASSED TO BE ENGROSSED as Amended by Committee Amendment "A" (H-626).
Sent for concurrence. ORDERED SENT FORTHWITH.
Reports Read
On motion by Senator CURRY of Waldo the Majority Ought to Pass as Amended Report ACCEPTED. PREVAILED.
Roll Call Ordered Roll Call Number 457 Yeas 19 - Nays 14 - Excused 2 - Absent 0
Bill READ ONCE.
Committee Amendment "A" (H-626) READ and ADOPTED.
Under suspension of the Rules, READ A SECOND TIME and PASSED TO BE ENGROSSED AS AMENDED by Committee Amendment "A" (H-626) in concurrence.
PASSED TO BE ENACTED.
Sent for concurrence. ORDERED SENT FORTHWITH.
PASSED TO BE ENACTED, in concurrence.

This Bill, having been returned by the Governor, together with objections to the same pursuant to Article IV, Part Third, Section 2 of the Constitution of the State of Maine, after reconsideration, the House proceeded to vote on the question: "Shall this Bill become a law notwithstanding the objections of the Governor?"

ROLL CALL NO. 587V 40 having voted in the affirmative and 100 in the negative, with 9 being absent and 2 excused, and accordingly it was the vote of the House that the Bill not become a law and the veto was sustained.
Placed in the Legislative Files. (DEAD)
